Exercises

Exercise Index

Each lesson includes hands-on exercises using the UW-Madison salary data. Solutions are provided in collapsible sections within each lesson.

Part 1: Foundations

Lesson Exercise Topic
1. Setup Create PyCharm project and run first script
2. Syntax Translate R snippets to Python
3. pandas Intro Load and explore salary data
4. Selection Filter salary data by multiple criteria

Part 2: Data Transformation

Lesson Exercise Topic
5. Aggregation Calculate median salaries and create pivot tables
6. Joining Merge salary and job metadata
7. Dates Analyze salary trends over time
8. Strings Clean division names

Part 3: Financial Analysis

Lesson Exercise Topic
9. Inflation Calculate real salary changes
10. Compensation Build peer comparison report
11. Workforce Analyze FTE changes
12. Tracking Employee journey analysis

Part 4: Visualization

Lesson Exercise Topic
13. Plotly Create salary visualization suite
14. Dashboards Build salary explorer dashboard

Part 5: Data Acquisition

Lesson Exercise Topic
15. APIs Build CPI data pipeline
16. Scraping Scrape job descriptions

Part 6: Production Patterns

Lesson Exercise Topic
17. Pipelines Refactor code to module
18. Capstone Complete analysis report

Tips for Success

  1. Try before looking - Attempt each exercise before viewing the solution
  2. Experiment - Modify the exercises to explore related questions
  3. Debug actively - Use PyCharm’s debugger to understand errors
  4. Ask questions - If something doesn’t make sense, revisit earlier lessons
Source Code
---
title: "Exercises"
---

## Exercise Index

Each lesson includes hands-on exercises using the UW-Madison salary data. Solutions are provided in collapsible sections within each lesson.

### Part 1: Foundations

| Lesson | Exercise Topic |
|--------|----------------|
| [1. Setup](../lessons/01-setup.qmd#exercise) | Create PyCharm project and run first script |
| [2. Syntax](../lessons/02-syntax.qmd#exercise) | Translate R snippets to Python |
| [3. pandas Intro](../lessons/03-pandas-intro.qmd#exercise) | Load and explore salary data |
| [4. Selection](../lessons/04-selection-filtering.qmd#exercise) | Filter salary data by multiple criteria |

### Part 2: Data Transformation

| Lesson | Exercise Topic |
|--------|----------------|
| [5. Aggregation](../lessons/05-reshaping-aggregation.qmd#exercise) | Calculate median salaries and create pivot tables |
| [6. Joining](../lessons/06-joining-merging.qmd#exercise) | Merge salary and job metadata |
| [7. Dates](../lessons/07-dates-timeseries.qmd#exercise) | Analyze salary trends over time |
| [8. Strings](../lessons/08-string-operations.qmd#exercise) | Clean division names |

### Part 3: Financial Analysis

| Lesson | Exercise Topic |
|--------|----------------|
| [9. Inflation](../lessons/09-inflation-adjustment.qmd#exercise) | Calculate real salary changes |
| [10. Compensation](../lessons/10-compensation-analysis.qmd#exercise) | Build peer comparison report |
| [11. Workforce](../lessons/11-workforce-analytics.qmd#exercise) | Analyze FTE changes |
| [12. Tracking](../lessons/12-tracking-individuals.qmd#exercise) | Employee journey analysis |

### Part 4: Visualization

| Lesson | Exercise Topic |
|--------|----------------|
| [13. Plotly](../lessons/13-plotly-basics.qmd#exercise) | Create salary visualization suite |
| [14. Dashboards](../lessons/14-interactive-dashboards.qmd#exercise) | Build salary explorer dashboard |

### Part 5: Data Acquisition

| Lesson | Exercise Topic |
|--------|----------------|
| [15. APIs](../lessons/15-working-with-apis.qmd#exercise) | Build CPI data pipeline |
| [16. Scraping](../lessons/16-web-scraping.qmd#exercise) | Scrape job descriptions |

### Part 6: Production Patterns

| Lesson | Exercise Topic |
|--------|----------------|
| [17. Pipelines](../lessons/17-reusable-pipelines.qmd#exercise) | Refactor code to module |
| [18. Capstone](../lessons/18-capstone.qmd) | Complete analysis report |

## Tips for Success

1. **Try before looking** - Attempt each exercise before viewing the solution
2. **Experiment** - Modify the exercises to explore related questions
3. **Debug actively** - Use PyCharm's debugger to understand errors
4. **Ask questions** - If something doesn't make sense, revisit earlier lessons
 

Built with Quarto | Data: UW-Madison Public Salary Records

  • Edit this page
  • Report an issue
